title: Enforce the seven gates (P1-P7) via the CLI

acceptanceCriteria.text: All gate tests pass (P1/P4/P5/P6/P7) via node --test

title: feat: chalk decisions

acceptanceCriteria.text: add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting it shows a decision after `chalk decision`

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk decisions` prints the contents of .chalk/decisions.md
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting it shows a decision after `chalk decision`

findings.note: `decisions()` calls readFileSync(s.p.decisions) without a guard; if .chalk/decisions.md were missing it would throw an unfriendly error. In practice init always creates the file, so both criteria are satisfied.

Test only covers the happy path (decision exists). No assertion of clean behavior when the log is empty/just-initialized, but the criterion only requires showing a decision after `chalk decision`, which is covered.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Add a tiny read-only `decisions` command to the `cmds` map in `bin/chalk.mjs` that prints `.chalk/decisions.md`. Mirror the existing `decision`/`lesson` pattern (`Store.open()` → `s.p.decisions`); `readFileSync` is already imported. Simplest, reuses existing store paths — no new utilities.

**Steps:**
1. In `bin/chalk.mjs`, after the `decision({...})` command (~line 875), add `decisions()` that does `const s = Store.open();` and `console.log(readFileSync(s.p.decisions, 'utf8'))` (guard with `existsSync` or fall back to empty — but `init` always creates the file, so a direct read is fine).
2. Add a one-line entry in `printHelp()` near the `chalk decision` line (~1011) for discoverability.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — new `decisions` cmd in the `cmds` object (reuse `Store.open()` + `s.p.decisions` from `lib/store.mjs`; `readFileSync` already imported); help text.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` — new test.

**Test:** Add `test('decisions — prints logged decisions', ...)`: `chalk(d,'init','--name','demo')`; `chalk(d,'decision','use sqlite for the ledger','--why','simplicity')`; then `const r = chalk(d,'decisions')`; assert `r.code===0` and `assert.match(r.out, /use sqlite for the ledger/)`. Fails today (unknown command exits 1), passes with the new command.

title: feat: chalk lesson list

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk lesson list` prints the recorded lessons from .chalk/lessons.md (the same memory injected into agents)
add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `lesson list` shows a previously-added lesson

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk lesson list` prints the recorded lessons from .chalk/lessons.md (the same memory injected into agents)
- [ ] `chalk lesson \"...\"` (adding) still works unchanged
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `lesson list` shows a previously-added lesson

findings.note: `chalk lesson list` reads s.lessons(1000) while context injection uses s.lessons(15); criterion 1 says 'the same memory injected into agents'. They share the source file but the cap differs, so list could surface older lessons not actually injected. Harmless/arguably more complete, not blocking.

Dispatch treats `_[0] === 'list'` as the list subcommand, so a literal lesson text starting with the word 'list' (e.g. `chalk lesson list the deps`) would be misrouted. Edge case, acceptable for the contract.
The added test only checks the happy path (one added lesson appears). No test for the empty-state ('no lessons recorded yet.') branch, but criteria 1-3 are all directly exercised.

plan:
## Approach
Add a `list` subcommand to the existing `lesson` handler in `bin/chalk.mjs`: if `_[0] === 'list'`, print recorded lessons via the existing `Store.lessons()`; otherwise keep the current add behavior unchanged. Reuses the store reader that already feeds agent context — minimal, no new module.

## Steps
1. In `bin/chalk.mjs` `lesson({ _, flags })`, branch at top: `if (_[0] === 'list')` → open store, get `s.lessons(1000)`, print each line (and a friendly note if empty), then `return`.
2. Leave the existing add path (`_.join(' ') || flags.text` → `appendLesson`) untouched so `chalk lesson "..."` still works.
3. Add usage line for `chalk lesson list` in the help block (~line 1019).

## Files
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `lesson()` handler (~line 885) + help text. Reuse `Store.open()` and `s.lessons()` from `lib/store.mjs`.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` — new test.

## Test
Add to `test/protocol.test.mjs`: `init`, `chalk(d,'lesson','prefer reusing existing utilities')` (code 0), then `chalk(d,'lesson','list')` — assert `.code === 0` and `.out` matches `/prefer reusing existing utilities/`. Fails today because `lesson list` currently treats "list" as lesson text and never prints stored lessons.

title: feat: chalk log --grep <text>

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk log --grep <text>` shows only update events whose title contains <text> (case-insensitive), still honoring `--n`, `--type`, and `--reverse`
add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `--grep` filters AND that it composes with another flag (e.g. `--type`)

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk log --grep <text>` shows only update events whose title contains <text> (case-insensitive), still honoring `--n`, `--type`, and `--reverse`
- [ ] without `--grep`, output is unchanged
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `--grep` filters AND that it composes with another flag (e.g. `--type`)

findings.note: The new test composes --grep with --type and verifies case-insensitivity + plain-unchanged, satisfying criterion 3. It does not assert --grep composes with --n or --reverse, but criterion 1's --n/--reverse behavior is structurally guaranteed (grep filters before slice(-n)/reverse, identical to the already-tested --type path).
String(u.title) coerces missing titles to 'undefined' which could spuriously match a grep for 'undefined', but update titles are always required in practice, so no realistic impact.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Mirror the existing `--type` filter in `cmds.log` (bin/chalk.mjs:921) — add a `--grep` filter on `u.title` applied to the full set *before* `.slice(-n)`, so `--n`/`--reverse`/`--json` continue to compose unchanged. Reusing the same filter slot is the smallest, pattern-consistent change.

**Steps:**
1. In `cmds.log`, after the `flags.type` filter line (925), add: `if (flags.grep) all = all.filter((u) => String(u.title).toLowerCase().includes(String(flags.grep).toLowerCase()));`
2. Leave the rest (slice, reverse, json/plain output) untouched — `--grep` automatically honors `--n`, `--type`, `--reverse`, `--json`.
3. Add the `--grep` token to the help line at bin/chalk.mjs:1028.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `cmds.log` (line ~925) + help text (line 1028). Reuse the existing `all.filter` chain pattern.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` — new test (append after the `--type` test ~line 117).

**Test:** Add `test('log --grep — filters by title substring (case-insensitive), composes with --type; plain unchanged', …)`:
- seed: `update 'deploy api'`, `update 'fix Login bug'`, `update 'deploy worker' --type milestone-hit`.
- `log --grep deploy` includes both deploy lines, excludes `fix Login bug`.
- case-insensitivity: `log --grep LOGIN` includes `fix Login bug`.
- composition: `log --grep deploy --type milestone-hit` shows only `deploy worker`, not `deploy api`.
- `log` (no `--grep`) still shows all three.

This fails today (the `grep` flag is ignored, so `--grep deploy` would show all events including `fix Login bug`) and passes with the filter added.
